name: Publish Release
on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main # Add a branch to which the app should be build and released or update the on:push in general
             # to be something more specialized, than a simple on:push.
      
jobs:
  publish-tauri:
    permissions:
      contents: write
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
    #   matrix:
    #     platform: ["demo"]
    #     # settings:
    #       # - platform: "macos-latest" # for Arm based macs (M1 and above).
    #         # args: "--target aarch64-apple-darwin"
    #       # - platform: "windows-latest"
    #       #   args: ""
    # runs-on:  ${{ matrix.platform }}
      matrix:
        settings:
          - platform: 'demo' # window-latest equivalent.

    runs-on: ${{ matrix.settings.platform }}

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          lfs: true
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable

      - name: Setup node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20 # lts/*

      - name: install frontend dependencies
        run: npm install # Change this to npm, pnpm or bun depending on which one you use.

      - name: Build and upload a GitHub release
        uses: tauri-apps/tauri-action@v0
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.PAT_FOR_PUBLIC_REPO }} # ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} # 
          TAURI_PRIVATE_KEY: ${{ secrets.TAURI_PRIVATE_KEY }}
          TAURI_KEY_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.TAURI_KEY_PASSWORD }}
        with:
          owner: PhysicalAddons
          repo: blenderbase-public
          tagName: v__VERSION__
          releaseName: "Blenderbase v__VERSION__" 
          # TODO add the documentation url here, so that users use a single source for it, rather than us trying to manage it in
          # several places.
          releaseBody: |
            Release notes: tba.
          releaseDraft: false # Maybe do this ("true") when building, and then change the release text post build.
          prerelease: false # Means that the release is available to people, but not production ready.
          includeUpdaterJson: true # If false, auto update will not work.
          releaseCommitish: main # This has to be the name of a branch in the target public repo, i.e. main branch in blenderbase-public-repo
